module-1-field-notes-repository.label: Module Project: Start Volunteer Project History
module-1-field-notes-repository.summary: Begin the cumulative volunteer-project project and record its first snapshot.

try_git_repo_inspection.title: Inspect the Volunteer Project Folder
try_git_repo_inspection.prompt: The parallel project opens inside `volunteer-project/`. Use the terminal to confirm the current directory and list its four plain-text files: `README.md`, `assignments.txt`, `meeting-times.txt`, and `old-notes.txt`. Do not initialize Git yet.
try_git_repo_inspection.hint: Confirm the folder and its small file set before changing repository state.

help.concept: A repository begins as an ordinary project folder.
help.hint_1: The terminal already opens inside volunteer-project.
help.hint_2: This step must leave the folder uninitialized.

try_git_repo_inspection.starterCode:
# Volunteer Project

Assignments and meeting times.
try_git_repo_inspection.instructions: The parallel project opens inside `volunteer-project/`. Use the terminal to confirm the current directory and list its four plain-text files: `README.md`, `assignments.txt`, `meeting-times.txt`, and `old-notes.txt`. Do not initialize Git yet.

zoeskoul_setup_sh.content:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -eu
rm -rf -- volunteer-project
mkdir -p -- volunteer-project
mkdir -p -- volunteer-project
printf %s '# Volunteer Project

Assignments and meeting times.
' > volunteer-project/README.md
mkdir -p -- volunteer-project
printf %s 'Volunteer assignments

Welcome table: Sam
Training desk: unassigned
' > volunteer-project/assignments.txt
mkdir -p -- volunteer-project
printf %s 'Volunteer meeting times

Orientation: Saturday 10:00 AM
' > volunteer-project/meeting-times.txt
mkdir -p -- volunteer-project
printf %s 'Old volunteer notes

Call list from last season.
' > volunteer-project/old-notes.txt

mkdir -p -- volunteer-project

Call list from last season.
' > volunteer-project/old-notes.txt

try_git_config.title: Initialize the Volunteer Project
try_git_config.prompt: The folder is ready and still has no repository metadata. Initialize `volunteer-project/` on `main` without changing any file.
try_git_config.hint: Create the repository in the current folder on `main`.

help.concept: The parallel project uses the same initialization skill in a different repository.

try_git_config.starterCode:
# Volunteer Project

Assignments and meeting times.
try_git_config.instructions: The folder is ready and still has no repository metadata. Initialize `volunteer-project/` on `main` without changing any file.

try_git_init.title: Configure the Volunteer Project Identity
try_git_init.prompt: Store the supplied author name `ZoeSkoul Learner` and email `learner@zoeskoul.local` in this repository only.
try_git_init.hint: Use the exact repository-local identity from the guided project.

help.concept: Each independent repository needs author identity before its first commit.

try_git_init.starterCode:
# Volunteer Project

Assignments and meeting times.
try_git_init.solutionCode:
git config --local user.name "ZoeSkoul Learner"
git config --local user.email "learner@zoeskoul.local"
try_git_init.instructions: Store the supplied author name `ZoeSkoul Learner` and email `learner@zoeskoul.local` in this repository only.

zoeskoul_setup_sh.content:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -eu
rm -rf -- volunteer-project
mkdir -p -- volunteer-project
git -C volunteer-project init -q -b main
mkdir -p -- volunteer-project
printf %s '# Volunteer Project

Assignments and meeting times.
' > volunteer-project/README.md
mkdir -p -- volunteer-project
printf %s 'Volunteer assignments

Welcome table: Sam
Training desk: unassigned

printf %s 'Volunteer meeting times

printf %s 'Old volunteer notes

Call list from last season.
' > volunteer-project/old-notes.txt

try_git_stage_files.title: Prepare the Volunteer Baseline
try_git_stage_files.prompt: The four project files are untracked. Stage `README.md`, `assignments.txt`, `meeting-times.txt`, and `old-notes.txt`, then inspect the staged summary before committing.
try_git_stage_files.hint: Include all four authored files in the first proposed snapshot.

help.concept: The module project reapplies staging without repeating the full lesson explanation.
help.hint_2: Do not stage hidden platform files.

try_git_stage_files.starterCode:
# Volunteer Project

Assignments and meeting times.
try_git_stage_files.solutionCode:
git add README.md assignments.txt meeting-times.txt old-notes.txt
git diff --staged --stat
try_git_stage_files.instructions: The four project files are untracked. Stage `README.md`, `assignments.txt`, `meeting-times.txt`, and `old-notes.txt`, then inspect the staged summary before committing.

#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -eu
rm -rf -- volunteer-project
mkdir -p -- volunteer-project
git -C volunteer-project init -q -b main
git -C volunteer-project config --local user.name 'ZoeSkoul Learner'
git -C volunteer-project config --local user.email learner@zoeskoul.local
mkdir -p -- volunteer-project
printf %s '# Volunteer Project

try_git_commit.title: Record and Verify the Volunteer Baseline
try_git_commit.prompt: Record the staged baseline with the commit message `Start volunteer project`. Then inspect the concise history and confirm that short status has no unfinished file entries.
try_git_commit.hint: Commit the prepared snapshot, then verify both history and current state.

help.concept: The parallel repository now has the canonical state used by the next module project.
help.hint_2: The final short status should be empty.

try_git_commit.starterCode:
# Volunteer Project

Assignments and meeting times.
try_git_commit.solutionCode:
git commit -m "Start volunteer project"
git log --oneline
git status --short
try_git_commit.instructions: Record the staged baseline with the commit message `Start volunteer project`. Then inspect the concise history and confirm that short status has no unfinished file entries.

moduleProject.title: Build the Volunteer Project Baseline
moduleProject.description: This project starts a second cumulative repository. It uses different files from the guided community-project work but requires the same Module 1 outcomes: inspect, initialize, configure, stage, commit, and verify. The final state continues into the Module 2 project.

intro-to-git-repository.title: Parallel Project: Volunteer Project
intro-to-git-repository.bodyMarkdown: `volunteer-project/` is a second small text-based project. It uses different names and content but asks you to reapply the same Module 1 workflow with less explanation. Its final state continues into the next module project.
